title: UPF3A

is a [protein](/wiki/Protein "Protein") that in humans is encoded by the *UPF3A* [gene](/wiki/Gene "Gene").[[4]](#cite_note-pmid11113196-4)[[5]](#cite_note-pmid11163187-5)[[6]](#cite_note-entrez-6)

| |  |  |  |  | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | [View/Edit Human](https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Q18045855 "d:Q18045855") | | [View/Edit Mouse](https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Q18262238 "d:Q18262238") | | | | | |

**Regulator of nonsense transcripts 3A** is a [protein](/wiki/Protein "Protein") that in humans is encoded by the *UPF3A* [gene](/wiki/Gene "Gene").[[4]](#cite_note-pmid11113196-4)[[5]](#cite_note-pmid11163187-5)[[6]](#cite_note-entrez-6)

This gene encodes a protein that is part of a post-splicing multiprotein complex involved in both mRNA nuclear export and [mRNA surveillance](/wiki/MRNA_surveillance "MRNA surveillance"). The encoded protein is one of two functional homologs to yeast Upf3p. mRNA surveillance detects exported mRNAs with truncated open reading frames and initiates [nonsense-mediated mRNA decay](/wiki/Nonsense-mediated_mRNA_decay "Nonsense-mediated mRNA decay") (NMD). When translation ends upstream from the last exon-exon junction, this triggers NMD to degrade mRNAs containing [premature stop codons](/wiki/Premature_stop_codon "Premature stop codon"). This protein binds to the mRNA and remains bound after nuclear export, acting as a [nucleocytoplasmic shuttling protein](/wiki/Nucleocytoplasmic_shuttling_protein "Nucleocytoplasmic shuttling protein"). It complexes with [RBM8A](/wiki/RBM8A "RBM8A") to bind specifically 20 nt upstream of exon-exon junctions. This gene is located on the long arm of chromosome 13. Two splice variants encoding different isoforms exist.[[6]](#cite_note-entrez-6)

## Interactions

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=UPF3A&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Interactions")]

UPF3A [interacts](/wiki/Protein-protein_interaction "Protein-protein interaction") with [RBM8A](/wiki/RBM8A "RBM8A"),[[7]](#cite_note-pmid11546873-7) [UPF2](/wiki/UPF2 "UPF2")[[5]](#cite_note-pmid11163187-5)[[8]](#cite_note-pmid14527413-8) and [UPF1](/wiki/UPF1 "UPF1").[[5]](#cite_note-pmid11163187-5)[[9]](#cite_note-pmid12723973-9)[[10]](#cite_note-pmid11544179-10)
